_comment: Pre-flight plan for uploading the curated ONNX artifacts under E:/Models to the
huggingface.co/Heliosoph organization. Each entry pairs a local folder with its
target HF repo, license obligations, and provenance.
1. Check the local folder has the expected files (see `expectedContents`).
2. Copy the matching template from models/upload-readmes/<repoSuffix>.md into
the local folder as README.md. Fill in any [BRACKETED] placeholders.
3. Drop the upstream LICENSE file(s) listed in `licenseFiles` into the folder.
6. Once uploaded, copy the commit sha from the HF web UI into models/catalog.json's
License confidence: 'high' = stated explicitly on the upstream HF model card.
'medium' = inferred from related models / org defaults; double-check.
All license assignments below need a human eyeball before upload.

provenance.precision: fp32 (also produced as fp16 by passing -Fp16)

license.notes: Inherits CreativeML OpenRAIL-M from base SD 1.5 + Lykon's AbsoluteReality terms. Hyper-SD LoRA is OpenRAIL-M (ByteDance). Preserve both.
Verify the model card on HF — epiCRealism's terms are inherited from SD 1.5 but the upstream repo sometimes lacks an explicit LICENSE file.
Nitrosocke models predate the OpenRAIL-M standard but are typically tagged as such. Verify.

provenance.schedulerHint: Euler, 4 steps, CFG=1; activator token 'mdjrny-v4 style'

provenance.vae: stabilityai/sd-vae-ft-mse (paired in during export — RV6 ships without VAE)

license.notes: Realistic Vision is more NSFW-permissive than the other Hyper variants. Consider noting that on the model card.

provenance.precision: fp32 — the optimum 1.24 / torch 2.4 / opset-14 fp16 path produces a numerically broken UNet for SDXL-class models. Revisit fp16 when a confirmed working toolchain exists.

license.notes: Inherits SDXL CreativeML OpenRAIL++-M from stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0.

uploads.exportedBy: manual optimum-cli export (Florence-2 uses custom modeling code; not in export-batch-onnx.ps1)

provenance.notes: Florence-2 ships as 4 separate ONNX files (vision_encoder / encoder / decoder / embed_tokens). All four must be present at load time.

provenance.exportToolchain: optimum-cli export onnx + post-export dynamic INT8 quantization
provenance.notes: Roughly half the disk + RAM of the fp16 variant; modest quality loss on text-heavy OCR prompts. Use this on CPU / mobile / NPU targets.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded from upstream Kokoro release; not converted locally
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder before upload: copy kokoro-v1.0.onnx + the entire kokoro-voices/ subfolder under one parent, then upload that parent.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX is the upstream's primary distribution format
provenance.notes: Voice bin files (af.bin, am_michael.bin, bf_emma.bin, etc.) are required at inference. 11 voices total: 6 American (af_*, am_*) + 4 British (bf_*, bm_*) + the default `af`.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded from Megvii's release; not converted locally
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy the 7 yolox_*.onnx files into one folder, then upload. Bundled into one repo so users get the full size ladder in one download.

provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — ONNX checkpoints published by Megvii
provenance.notes: AGPL-free alternative to YOLOv8. Same 80-class COCO labels. Recommend yolox_s for general use, yolox_nano for CPU/edge, yolox_x for max accuracy.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Meta SAM mask decoders + ChaoningZhang/MobileSAM image encoder
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy the 3 SAM files into one folder, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: Meta SAM (mask decoders) + ChaoningZhang/MobileSAM (ViT-T image encoder)
provenance.notes: MobileSAM ViT-T encoder (~27 MB) pairs with either SAM mask decoder variant (single-mask or multi-mask output). Together they form a complete SAM pipeline at ~60 MB total — orders of magnitude smaller than original SAM ViT-H.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Intel ISL DPT-Large monocular depth estimator
uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy dpt_large_384.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.notes: Monocular depth estimation, 384×384 input, ~330M params. Outputs a single-channel depth map (relative depth, not metric).

uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy ch_PP-OCRv4_det.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.baseModel: PaddlePaddle PP-OCRv4 detection model (Chinese-tuned, also works on English)
provenance.notes: Text-region detector — input to an OCR recognizer (TrOCR / PP-OCRv4 recognizer / Florence-2 OCR). Outputs polygon coordinates for each detected text region. Despite the 'ch_' prefix, detection is largely language-agnostic — the script-specific weights live in the recognizer, not the detector.

license.notes: Baidu / Chinese-affiliated source — Heliosoph mirror is a trade-stability hedge.

uploads.uploadStaging: Stage to a temp folder: copy realesr-general-x4v3.onnx into one folder, then upload.

provenance.notes: Tiny (~5 MB), 4× resolution upscale on general images (photos, illustrations, screenshots). The 'general-x4v3' variant is the latest generic-purpose model — separate anime / face variants exist upstream if needed later.

license.notes: Chinese-affiliated author — Heliosoph mirror is a trade-stability hedge.

uploads.exportedBy: downloaded — Qualcomm AI Hub release of MediaPipe Face (Apache-2.0 ONNX via zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch)
uploads.uploadStaging: Two-step staging. (1) Download both zips: float = https://qaihub-public-assets.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/qai-hub-models/models/mediapipe_face/releases/v0.53.1/mediapipe_face-onnx-float.zip ; int8 = https://qaihub-public-assets.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/qai-hub-models/models/mediapipe_face/releases/v0.53.1/mediapipe_face-onnx-w8a8.zip . (2) Unzip each. Rename the extracted directories to float/ and int8/, place both inside a single staging folder. (3) Drop README.md + LICENSE in the staging root. (4) Upload that staging folder.

provenance.baseModel: Google MediaPipe Face Detection (BlazeFace) + Face Landmark (6-point)
provenance.exportToolchain: n/a — Qualcomm AI Hub ONNX export via zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch PyTorch port
provenance.precision: float (fp32) + W8A8 (INT8 dynamic) — both shipped as subfolders
provenance.notes: Two-model pipeline: face_detector finds bounding boxes; face_landmark_detector returns 6 keypoints per face (eyes, nose tip, mouth, eye tragions). External weights pattern — each .onnx has a .data sibling that must travel with it. ~3 MB total per variant.

license.notes: License chain: Google MediaPipe (Apache-2.0) → zmurez/MediaPipePyTorch port (Apache-2.0) → Qualcomm AI Hub ONNX export (Apache-2.0). Google trained on commercial-friendly data; clean for any use.

rejected.reason: Ultralytics YOLOv8 is AGPL-3.0; redistributing under Heliosoph/ would impose AGPL obligations on downstream consumers. Use yolox-onnx bundle instead — same task, Apache-2.0.
Meta MusicGen is CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial only). Cannot mirror under Heliosoph/ without implying commercial use is OK. Catalog should reference Meta's repo directly if used at all.

Mirroring is legally permitted under the Stability AI Community License / OpenRAIL++-M, but they're high-traffic models that benefit more from being pointed at the upstream pre-converted ONNX (when available). Revisit if upstream ONNX is missing or stale.
All have first-party or onnx-community / Xenova mirrors on HF already. Catalog references those instead of mirroring.

InsightFace's umbrella policy restricts ALL released model weights to non-commercial research use, rooted in the WIDER FACE dataset's academic-only terms. The SCRFD source code is Apache-2.0 (OpenMMLab) but the trained weights are encumbered. Replaced with mediapipe-face-onnx (Google MediaPipe via Qualcomm, Apache-2.0, trained on commercial-friendly data).
